Saw this film today. The Philippou brothers somehow managed to create a follow up to one of the best horror films of the last several decades-TALK TO ME-even MORE harrowing, unsettling, emotional, and disturbing. BRING HER BACK is an instant classic; no debate. Everything about this film is pure perfection. It will stay with you for days, if not weeks. A true masterpiece and one I will be thinking about A LOT. I cannot wait to see what Danny and Michael have on tap next. These bros are experts at making TRUE horror films that are unforgettable and traumatizing. Sheer genius.
I feel the exact same way. I can’t believe they managed to to surpass talk to me

I'm not a massive horror fan, mainly because they all follow a similar thread (eg kid draws black crayon demons, or demonic nuns etc) so when I saw Talk To Me, it was incredible. I couldn't believe it was filmed about 2 suburbs from where I lived lol. It's also the first time after watching a horror, that I researched the directos to see what flick they're doing next, and this short trailer gave me goosebumps. I have a feeling these 2 talented Aussies will be around for a while, hopefully doing more cutting-edge, original horrors for many years, as this genre needs it!

I’m sorry but the movie absolutely lacked in my opinion… now I’m a movie head and even though I don’t care for satanic movies I always give every movie a fair go but this wasn’t even satanic it seemed like the opposite almost like an alien type of movie invasion of the body snatcher vibes… the gore was good haven’t seen any good gore lately so the kid eating the knife got me pretty good but the overall story was very blah and a lot of filler time as in a ton of 10sec scenes of people just staring out of a window or staring at a wall or staring at another person and when you do that a number of times it adds up to 20min of the entire movie… no scenery… everything took place at an air bnb or something it’s like they never left the same set more than once so the movie lacked in scenes basically making the entire movie out of the same scene… i give it a 6/10 and usually I give a 4 just for effort of making a movie but 1 point for gore and 1 point for using a blind actor it added some originality. I think these guys could do better if they opened up the budget.
